story.label: My Story
story.heading: From the beginning. Every rep. Still going.
story.body1: I'm Kim — LA-based, Latina, and obsessed with what the body is capable of.
story.body2: It started in 2018 with zero experience and a lot of heart. No trainer, no plan — just showing up. Over the years I built strength, confidence, and a community of women who actually want to see real results.
story.body3: My thing is glutes and legs. Heavy, intentional, consistent. If you're here for the transformation — física y mental — you're in the right place.

philosophy.heading: Training Philosophy
philosophy.body: Heavy, intentional, and consistent. Every session is built around progressive overload — tracking personal lifts, competing with your past self, and showing up with purpose. No shortcuts. Solo disciplina.

forWho.heading: Who This Is For
forWho.body: Women 18–35 who are done with generic content and want real, relatable fitness — strength-focused, no filters, no fads. If you're ready to build something lasting, you're in the right place.

pillars.title: Glutes & Legs
pillars.description: The foundation of my training. Hip thrusts, Bulgarian splits, RDLs — built for results.

pillars.title: Progressive Overload
pillars.description: Track your lifts. Beat your last set. Compete with yesterday's you.

pillars.title: Real Content
pillars.description: No filters, no fads. Just consistent effort and honest results.

tiktok.label: TikTok
tiktok.heading: Watch the Videos
tiktok.subheading: Training clips, tips, and real gym content.

emailSignup.subheading: Real training, real food, no fluff — straight to your inbox.

workouts.pageDescription: Kim's full training split — glutes, pull, legs, push, and cardio. Built for real results.
workouts.heading: My Training Split
workouts.subheading: A glute-focused, upper-body-balanced rotation with one rest day per week.

gluteDay.title: Glute Day
gluteDay.description: Focused on glute development, lower-body strength, and shaping. Starts with the strongest glute-dominant movement and progresses through unilateral work, hinge patterns, and accessory movements.

exercises.name: Hip Thrusts
exercises.note: Lead with your strongest glute movement — full range, controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squats
exercises.note: Unilateral work for stability and balanced glute development.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlifts
exercises.note: Posterior chain focus — glutes and hamstrings with hip hinge.

exercises.name: Adductor Machine
exercises.note: Inner thigh targeting for complete lower-body development.

exercises.name: Abductor Machine
exercises.note: Outer glute focus — builds width and shape.

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.note: Round out the session with lower leg work.

pullDay.title: Pull Day
pullDay.description: Focuses on the back, rear shoulder support, posture, and upper-body pulling strength. Includes a 6-minute ab circuit.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldowns
exercises.note: Vertical pulling — builds back width and lat development.

exercises.name: Rows
exercises.note: Horizontal pulling — thickness and mid-back strength.

exercises.name: Face Pulls with Rope
exercises.note: Occasional — rear delts and upper back support.

exercises.name: 6-Minute Ab Circuit
exercises.note: 45s on / 15s off: Scissors, Upper Ab Crunches, Leg & Chest Lifts, Side Oblique Crunches.

legsGlutesDay.title: Legs with Glute Focus
legsGlutesDay.description: Still glute-involved, but places more emphasis on overall leg development. Combines glute activation with heavier compound leg work.

exercises.name: Hip Thrusts
exercises.note: Glute activation before heavier compound work.

exercises.name: Squats
exercises.note: Full lower-body stimulus — the foundation of leg day.

exercises.name: Leg Extensions
exercises.note: Quad isolation — controlled squeeze at the top.

exercises.name: Lying Hamstring Curls
exercises.note: Hamstring isolation for balanced leg development.

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.note: Complete the session with lower leg work.

pushDay.title: Push Day
pushDay.description: Focused on chest, shoulders, and triceps. Machine-based pressing, chest isolation, and shoulder accessory work.

exercises.name: Push Machine
exercises.note: Primary pressing movement — chest and front delt focus.

exercises.name: Fly Machine
exercises.note: Chest isolation — stretch and squeeze through full range.

exercises.name: Incline Chest Press
exercises.note: Every other push day — upper chest emphasis.

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdowns
exercises.note: Arm development and pressing support.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raises
exercises.note: Shoulder width — controlled, no momentum.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Front Raises
exercises.note: Occasional — front delt variation.

cardio.heading: Cardio
cardio.subheading: Every other training day, after lifting. Rotate through these options:
cardio.note: Cardio is optional but consistent — part of the overall routine, not a punishment.

overloadNote.heading: Progressive Overload
overloadNote.body: Weights are not prescribed. Track your own lifts, record your performance, and focus on improving against your past self. Compete with yesterday's you — not anyone else.

diet.pageDescription: Kim's daily nutrition structure — high-protein, balanced meals, and recovery principles.
diet.heading: Daily Nutrition
diet.subheading: Simple, balanced, and repeatable. Built to support training, recovery, and long-term results.

breakfast.heading: Breakfast
breakfast.description: A strong start with protein, light carbohydrates, and nutrient-dense foods.

lunch.heading: Lunch
lunch.description: Centered around a primary protein source, a carbohydrate for energy, and vegetables.

dinner.heading: Dinner
dinner.description: Similar structure to lunch, slightly smaller portions depending on hunger and activity.

principles.heading: Nutrition Principles
items: Include carbohydrates to support energy and training
Adjust portions based on goals and activity level

recovery.heading: Recovery Is Part of the Plan
recovery.subheading: Nutrition alone isn't recovery. Real results come from treating rest as seriously as training.

pillars.title: Nutrition
pillars.body: Food is fuel and repair. High-protein meals support muscle growth and help you show up strong every session.

pillars.title: Jacuzzi
pillars.body: Active recovery. Heat helps relax muscles, reduce soreness, and bring the body back down after intense training.

pillars.title: Sleep
pillars.body: Non-negotiable. Your body builds during rest — not during the workout. Protect your sleep like a training priority.

pillars.title: Water
pillars.body: Hydration affects everything: performance, recovery, focus. Drink consistently throughout the day.

startOver.pageTitle: What I Would Do If I Started Over
startOver.pageDescription: Kim's honest advice for fitness beginners — what she'd do differently from day one.
startOver.heading: What I Would Do If I Started Over
startOver.intro: If I had to start my fitness journey over again, I would make things much simpler from the beginning. I would stop chasing perfect routines, random trends, and unrealistic expectations — and I would focus on doing the basics well for long enough to actually see progress.

sections.heading: Form Before Weight
sections.body: The first thing I would do is focus on form before trying to lift heavy. A lot of people rush into increasing weight too quickly, but if your form isn't solid, you're not getting the full benefit of the exercise. Before worrying about how heavy the weight is, I would focus on learning the movement correctly, controlling the tempo, and understanding exactly what muscle I'm trying to target.

sections.heading: Build the Mind-Muscle Connection
sections.body: I would make sure to build a real mind-muscle connection first. That means learning how to actually feel the muscle working during each exercise rather than just moving weight from point A to point B. Especially for glute and leg training, this matters a lot. If you can't feel the glutes working, adding more weight isn't always the answer. The better approach is often to reduce the weight, clean up the form, slow down the movement, and build better control first.

sections.heading: Lift Heavier Only After the Basics Are In Place
sections.body: I would also remind myself that lifting heavier is only useful once the basics are already in place. Progressive overload matters, but it works best when built on proper mechanics. Lightweight with perfect form is more valuable than heavier weight with poor control. Once the movement is stable, the muscle is being properly targeted, and the technique is consistent — then it makes sense to gradually push the weight forward.

sections.heading: Stop Changing the Routine
sections.body: If I had to start over, I would also stop changing my routine too often. I would choose a structured split, stay consistent, and give the plan enough time to work. I would track my workouts, record my lifts, and focus on improving my own performance over time instead of comparing myself to other people.

sections.heading: Keep Nutrition Simple
sections.body: With nutrition, I would keep things simple and repeatable. I would focus on protein, balanced meals, and consistency rather than trying to eat perfectly all the time. Results come from doing the fundamentals consistently — not from being extreme for a short period of time.

sections.heading: Be More Patient
sections.body: Most importantly, I would be more patient. Real progress takes time. Strength, confidence, body composition, and visible development all come from repeated effort over weeks and months — not from trying to rush the process.

priorities.heading: Quick Tips
items: Use lighter weight until technique is solid
Progressively overload once the basics are in place
Stay consistent with a structured split
Compete with my past self, not other people

workWithMe.pageDescription: Partner with Kimberly Vanessa — Latina fitness creator, glutes & legs specialist, 2.2K IG followers.
workWithMe.heading: Work With Me
workWithMe.subheading: Authentic content. Real audience. Serious results.

about.heading: About Me
about.body: I'm a Latina fitness creator based in Los Angeles, CA. I specialize in glute and leg training and have been documenting my strength journey since 2018. With 2.2K Instagram followers and 33K on TikTok, my audience is women 18–35 who are serious about building strength and looking for real, relatable content — not filters and fads.

items.description: Exercise demos, full training sessions, and technique breakdowns.
Progress updates, before/after comparisons, and honest journey storytelling.
Meal prep, daily routines, and sustainable habit content.
Gym wear, supplements, and equipment she actually uses.

items.title: Authentic Audience
items.description: My followers are real — not inflated. Women who follow me are genuinely invested in fitness and ready to act on recommendations.

items.title: Consistent Posting
items.description: I post regularly across Instagram and TikTok. Consistent presence means consistent exposure for your brand.

items.title: High-Intent Niche
items.description: Glutes & legs is a high-purchase-intent niche. My audience actively buys gym wear, supplements, and equipment.

items.title: Bilingual Creator
items.description: I create content in both English and Spanish — reaching a broader Latina and U.S. Hispanic audience.

sections.heading: Information We Collect
sections.body: We collect information you voluntarily provide, such as your email address when you sign up for our newsletter. We may also collect basic analytics data (pages visited, session duration) through Google Analytics.

sections.heading: How We Use Your Information
sections.body: Your email address is used solely to send you newsletters and fitness content from Kimberly Vanessa. We do not sell or share your personal information with third parties for marketing purposes.

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.body: This site uses a language preference cookie to remember your language choice. We also use Google Analytics cookies for site usage analysis. You can disable cookies in your browser settings.

sections.heading: Third-Party Services
sections.body: We use Google Analytics for analytics. Their privacy policy applies to data collected by their service. We use Resend to manage email subscriptions.

sections.heading: Your Rights
sections.body: You may unsubscribe from our newsletter at any time via the link in any email. To request deletion of your data, contact us at kimberlyvanessagym@gmail.com.

sections.heading: Acceptance of Terms
sections.body: By accessing kimberlyvanessa.com, you agree to these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, please do not use this site.

sections.heading: Content
sections.body: All content on this site — including workout plans, nutrition information, photos, and written content — is for informational purposes only. It is not medical or professional fitness advice. Consult a qualified professional before starting any fitness or nutrition program.

sections.heading: Intellectual Property
sections.body: All content, images, and branding on this site are the property of Kimberly Vanessa. You may not reproduce, distribute, or use them without explicit written permission.

sections.heading: Limitation of Liability
sections.body: Kimberly Vanessa is not liable for any injuries, losses, or damages resulting from use of information on this site. Use all fitness and nutrition content at your own risk.

sections.heading: Changes
sections.body: We reserve the right to update these terms at any time. Continued use of the site after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated terms.

sections.heading: Contact
sections.body: Questions about these terms? Email kimberlyvanessagym@gmail.com.
